title: Fixes bug whereby read_only bool is called as a function
body:
This is a simple bug fix to remedy the following issue:

```python
  File ".../site-packages/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py", line 374, in emacs_insert_mode
    or app.current_buffer.read_only()
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Exception 'bool' object is not callable
```

It would appear to be a simple typo, calling the boolean `read_only` property as if it were a function.

step_plan.summary: Make predicates in src/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py tolerant to both callable and boolean Buffer.read_only by altering is_read_only to handle both cases and updating all mode/predicate functions to use this robust check to avoid 'bool object is not callable' exceptions.

steps.operation: Make is_read_only robust to both callable and boolean read_only values
steps.reason: Currently is_read_only (and other predicates) call current_buffer.read_only() assuming it is callable. Some Buffer implementations expose read_only as a plain bool/property, causing "'bool' object is not callable". Modify is_read_only to inspect buffer.read_only: call it if callable, otherwise coerce to bool. Implement the small helper logic here (either as an internal helper function or inside is_read_only) so that all callers can rely on a single correct behavior.
steps.side_effects: Behavior is backward-compatible: callers of is_read_only will receive the same boolean semantics regardless of whether buffer.read_only is a callable filter or a boolean. Tests that exercise mode detection must be run/updated. Any direct callers that expected a callable read_only (rare) should not be affected because we only read/evaluate the value. Minimal API surface change since this is internal logic in a predicate.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in vi_navigation_mode
steps.reason: vi_navigation_mode currently uses app.current_buffer.read_only() in its predicate expression. Replace that call with the robust is_read_only-based check to avoid the 'bool object is not callable' exception when read_only is a boolean.
steps.side_effects: Fixes runtime exceptions during mode detection and key-binding selection. Update/execute unit tests for Vi mode detection. No public API change, but behavior now tolerates both callable and boolean read_only; dependent code that relied on an exception will be affected only insofar as it previously crashed.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in vi_insert_mode
steps.reason: vi_insert_mode contains guards that call current_buffer.read_only(). Use the centralized robust check (is_read_only/_helper) so the predicate works when read_only is a bool as well as when it is callable.
steps.side_effects: Prevents crashes when entering Vi insert mode for buffers where read_only is a boolean. Requires running mode-detection tests. No change to public APIs; downstream key-bindings that depend on vi_insert_mode will benefit from the fix.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in vi_insert_multiple_mode
steps.reason: vi_insert_multiple_mode currently assumes read_only is callable. Updating it to use the robust helper prevents "'bool' object is not callable" exceptions for buffers exposing read_only as a boolean.
steps.side_effects: Improves stability for multi-cursor/multiple-insert modes when used with buffers that have boolean read_only. Tests covering multi-insert mode should be executed/added. No breaking API changes.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in vi_replace_mode
steps.reason: vi_replace_mode uses read_only() in guards. Replace with the helper to support both callable and boolean read_only, preventing the callable-bool exception during replace-mode detection.
steps.side_effects: Fixes potential crashes entering Vi replace mode for buffers with boolean read_only. Run/adjust replace-mode tests. No public API change; behavior is preserved while becoming more tolerant.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in vi_replace_single_mode
steps.reason: vi_replace_single_mode currently calls read_only() and will raise if read_only is a boolean. Use the shared robust check to support both boolean and callable read_only values.
steps.side_effects: Prevents runtime exceptions for single-character replace operations on boolean-read-only buffers. Requires running tests that cover single replace mode. No API break; internal predicate behavior becomes more resilient.

steps.operation: Replace direct call to app.current_buffer.read_only() with the robust check in emacs_insert_mode
steps.reason: The reported exception arises from emacs_insert_mode calling app.current_buffer.read_only() when read_only is a boolean. Modify this predicate to use the robust helper/is_read_only so it handles both callable and plain boolean read_only values.
steps.side_effects: Fixes the observed "'bool' object is not callable" crash when using Emacs insert mode with buffers that expose read_only as a boolean. Mode detection tests should be run; behavior remains functionally equivalent while being tolerant to different Buffer implementations.
